step_by_step_thinking:
Herpes Zoster typically presents unilaterally due to reactivation of latent varicella-zoster virus along the distribution of a single dorsal root ganglion. It causes severe neuralgia during the rash phase and post-herpetic neuralgia after resolution of the rash. Vesicle crusts usually appear later in the course of the illness.
\boxed{A} Based on the characteristics mentioned, unilateral occurrence is a distinguishing feature of Herpes Zoster compared to other vesico-bullous eruptions like impetigo or contact dermatitis, which can occur bilaterally without strict adherence to nerve distributions. Therefore, option A correctly identifies the key distinction.

\boxed{A} Based on the characteristics mentioned, unilateral occurrence is a distinguishing feature of Herpes Zoster compared to other vesico-bullous eruptions like impetigo or contact dermatitis, which can occur bilaterally without strict adherence to nerve distributions. Therefore, option A correctly identifies the key distinction. \boxed{A}
